# Zro2One

> AI education for everyone — from essential to research depth.

## AI Foundations

- [Attention Mechanisms: The Core of Modern AI](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-foundations-attention-mechanisms): Attention is the single most important idea in modern AI. This guide explains how it works, why it was a breakthrough, and what it enables that previous approaches couldn't.
- [From RNNs to Transformers: The Architecture Shift That Changed AI](https://zro2.one/learn/deep-learning-rnns-and-transformers): Why did transformers replace RNNs so completely? Understanding the problems with recurrent architectures reveals exactly why attention-based transformers were such a breakthrough.
- [Ensemble Methods Explained: Bagging, Boosting, and Random Forests](https://zro2.one/learn/machine-learning-ensemble-methods): Ensemble methods combine multiple models to produce better predictions than any single model. Here's how bagging, boosting, and random forests actually work.
- [Audio-Visual Multimodal Models: How They Work and What They Can Do](https://zro2.one/learn/mllms-audio-visual-models): The next frontier for MLLMs isn't just text + images — it's audio and video. Here's how audio-visual models work and what capabilities they enable.
- [Named Entity Recognition: From Rules to Neural Networks](https://zro2.one/learn/nlp-named-entity-recognition): Named entity recognition is one of NLP's fundamental tasks. This guide covers how NER evolved, how modern neural approaches work, and how to use it in practice.
- [AI Safety: What It Is and Why It Matters](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-ai-safety): AI safety is one of the most discussed and least understood topics in AI. Here's a clear explanation of what it actually means, why researchers take it seriously, and what's being done about it.
- [AI Foundations: Bias–Variance Tradeoff Without the Math Panic](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-foundations-bias-variance-intuition): A plain-language explanation of bias, variance, and why model quality depends on balancing both.
- [AI Glossary for Operators — 35 Terms You’ll See in Real Deployments](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-glossary-operators-edition): An operator-focused glossary of practical AI terms across models, infrastructure, evaluation, and governance.
- [Getting Started with AI — A 7-Day Plan That Builds Real Skill](https://zro2.one/learn/getting-started-ai-first-7-days-plan): A beginner-friendly one-week AI learning plan focused on practical outcomes, not theory overload.
- [NLP from Rules to Transformers — What Changed and What Still Matters](https://zro2.one/learn/nlp-from-rules-to-transformers-guide): A concise history of NLP evolution and the enduring principles teams still need for modern language systems.
- [What Is AI? A Practical Definition for Business Leaders in 2026](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-ai-for-business-leaders-2026): A clear, non-hype explanation of AI for decision-makers, with boundaries, capabilities, and implementation implications.
- [Transformers: The Architecture Behind Modern AI](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-foundations-transformers): Transformers are the architecture behind GPT, BERT, Gemini, and essentially every modern AI system. Here's how they actually work — the attention mechanism, positional encoding, and training.
- [AI Glossary: Essential Terms for Beginners — From Algorithm to Zero-Shot](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-glossary-beginner): Clear, jargon-free definitions of the most important AI terms. If you're new to AI and keep running into words you don't know, start here.
- [Convolutional Neural Networks: How AI Learned to See](https://zro2.one/learn/deep-learning-cnns-explained): CNNs are the architecture that gave AI the ability to recognize images. Here's how convolutions work, why pooling matters, and how the architecture evolved from LeNet to ResNet.
- [Temperature, Top-P, and Sampling: The Knobs That Control LLM Creativity](https://zro2.one/learn/llms-temperature-and-sampling-explained): Temperature and sampling parameters control how creative or predictable your LLM's outputs are. Here's what they actually do and how to use them.
- [The Bias-Variance Tradeoff: Why ML Models Fail in Two Opposite Ways](https://zro2.one/learn/machine-learning-bias-variance-tradeoff): The bias-variance tradeoff is the central tension in machine learning. Understanding it explains why models overfit, underfit, and how to find the sweet spot.
- [When AI Reads Images and Text Together: Multimodal AI Explained](https://zro2.one/learn/multimodal-ai-how-it-works): ChatGPT can now see photos. Gemini can watch videos. This is multimodal AI — AI that processes more than just text. Here's how it works and why it matters, explained simply.
- [Sentiment Analysis: How AI Understands Tone and Opinion](https://zro2.one/learn/nlp-sentiment-analysis-explained): Sentiment analysis — detecting positive, negative, or nuanced emotion in text — is one of the most widely deployed NLP tasks. Here's how it works, what it can and can't do, and how to use it.
- [Narrow AI vs. General AI: What's the Real Difference?](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-ai-narrow-vs-general): ChatGPT is impressive, but it's not general AI. Understanding the difference between narrow AI and artificial general intelligence helps you cut through the hype and understand what AI can actually do.
- [Neural Networks: The Architecture That Powers Modern AI](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-foundations-neural-networks): Neural networks are the engine behind virtually every AI breakthrough of the past decade. Here's how they work, why they work, and what makes them so powerful — explained without the math.
- [AI Glossary: Advanced Terms — From Attention to Zero-Shot](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-glossary-advanced): A comprehensive reference for intermediate-to-advanced AI terminology. Covers attention mechanisms, fine-tuning types, evaluation metrics, agentic AI, alignment concepts, and more.
- [How Neural Networks Actually Learn: Backpropagation Explained](https://zro2.one/learn/deep-learning-backpropagation): Backpropagation is the algorithm that makes deep learning work. Here's a clear technical explanation of how gradients flow backward through a network, why it works, and what actually happens during training.
- [Vision-Language Models: How MLLMs Understand Images and Text Together](https://zro2.one/learn/mllms-vision-language-models): A technical deep dive into multimodal large language models (MLLMs) — how vision encoders connect to language models, what architectural choices matter, and how capability limits manifest in practice.
- [Modern NLP: How Language Understanding Works in 2026](https://zro2.one/learn/nlp-modern-landscape): A technical survey of modern NLP — from foundational tasks and pre-transformer approaches to the transformer revolution, current SOTA, and where the field is heading in 2026.
- [What Is AI in 2026? The Definitive Guide for Right Now](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-ai-2026-update): The AI landscape changes fast. Here's a clear, current picture of what AI is, what it can do in 2026, and what actually matters for understanding where things stand right now.
- [AI Map — How ML, Deep Learning, NLP, LLMs, and MLLMs Fit Together](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-map-how-ml-dl-llm-fit): A clear visual map of AI and where ML, DL, NLP, LLMs, and MLLMs sit inside it.
- [Audio AI — Frontier Research and Unresolved Problems](https://zro2.one/learn/audio-ai-research): A research-level map of where audio AI actually stands: speech synthesis, recognition robustness, music generation, audio understanding, and the hard problems that remain.
- [Image AI — Frontier Research and Unresolved Problems](https://zro2.one/learn/image-ai-research): Where image AI research actually stands: diffusion model frontiers, computer vision robustness, generative limits, evaluation methodology, and the hardest remaining problems.
- [Machine Learning — The Plain-English Guide](https://zro2.one/learn/machine-learning-essential): What machine learning is, what it is not, and why it works — explained with zero jargon.
- [Video AI — Frontier Research and Unresolved Problems](https://zro2.one/learn/video-ai-research): A research-level examination of video AI: generation frontiers, understanding challenges, temporal modeling limits, and why video is harder than images in ways that matter.
- [What is Deep Learning? (Without the Hype)](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-deep-learning-essential): Deep learning explained clearly: what it is, why it works, and where it fits.
- [What is an LLM?](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-llm-essential): A simple explanation of large language models and what they are actually doing.
- [What is an MLLM? (Multimodal LLM)](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-mllm-essential): MLLMs explained: models that understand and generate across text, images, audio, and more.
- [What is NLP? (Natural Language Processing)](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-nlp-essential): NLP explained simply: how computers work with human language.
- [The AI Glossary: Every Term You Actually Need to Know](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-glossary): AI has a jargon problem. Here's every term you'll encounter — defined in plain English, with context for why it matters.
- [How LLMs Work — What It Means for How You Use Them](https://zro2.one/learn/how-llms-work-applied): Understanding how LLMs work under the hood makes you dramatically better at using them. Here's what every professional needs to know.
- [How LLMs Work — The Plain English Version](https://zro2.one/learn/how-llms-work-essential): Large language models power ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Here's how they actually work — no jargon, no math, just clear explanations.
- [How LLMs Work — Open Problems and Frontier Research](https://zro2.one/learn/how-llms-work-research): The frontier of LLM research: scaling laws, emergent capabilities, mechanistic interpretability, reasoning limitations, and where the field is heading.
- [How LLMs Work — The Transformer Architecture Explained](https://zro2.one/learn/how-llms-work-technical): A technical deep-dive into transformer architecture, attention mechanisms, training pipelines, and the engineering decisions that make modern LLMs work.
- [What is AI? (And What It Isn't)](https://zro2.one/learn/what-is-ai): AI is everywhere, but most explanations are either hype or jargon. Here's a clear, honest explanation of what artificial intelligence actually is — and isn't.

## This Week in AI

- [This Week in AI](https://zro2.one/learn/this-week-in-ai-007): This week: AI agents move from demos to production deployments, the cost curve keeps falling, and the open-source ecosystem closes the gap with frontier models.
- [This Week in AI](https://zro2.one/learn/this-week-in-ai-006): A practical weekly briefing on what mattered most in AI: reliability tooling, model economics, and enterprise deployment patterns.
- [This Week in AI](https://zro2.one/learn/this-week-in-ai-005): Week 5: Agentic AI hits real-world friction at scale, the frontier model race accelerates with a surprise entrant, and AI in K-12 education becomes a genuine policy flashpoint.
- [This Week in AI](https://zro2.one/learn/this-week-in-ai-004): This week: the reasoning model race heats up, open source closes the gap faster than anyone expected, and the US government finally starts asking serious governance questions. Here's what happened and why it matters.
- [This Week in AI](https://zro2.one/learn/this-week-in-ai-001): A signal-over-noise digest: what changed in AI this week, what to ignore, and what to test.
- [This Week in AI](https://zro2.one/learn/this-week-in-ai-002): Week two of the digest: where AI is creating real leverage and where teams are still wasting cycles.
- [This Week in AI](https://zro2.one/learn/this-week-in-ai-003): AI went to war — literally. This week's digest covers the Anthropic-Pentagon crisis, OpenAI's military deal, a new model update, and what it all means for the industry.

## Practice & Tools

- [AI Note-Taking and Knowledge Management Tools in 2026](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-tools-knowledge-management-2026): The AI note-taking landscape has matured. Here's what's actually useful, what's hype, and how to build a knowledge system that works with AI instead of around it.
- [AI-Assisted Customer Support: A Practical Workflow Guide](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-workflows-customer-support): Customer support is one of the most mature AI deployment domains. Here's how high-performing teams structure their AI workflows — including the parts that are easy to get wrong.
- [AI Music Generation in 2026: Tools, Techniques, and Honest Limits](https://zro2.one/learn/audio-ai-music-generation-2026): AI music generation has matured into a genuine creative tool. Here's what it can do, where it still struggles, and how to actually get good results.
- [Your First AI Project: A Step-by-Step Guide](https://zro2.one/learn/getting-started-first-ai-project): Stop reading about AI and start building something. Here's a practical guide to choosing and completing your first AI project — with real suggestions, not vague advice.
- [AI Image Editing: Tools and Techniques That Actually Work](https://zro2.one/learn/image-ai-editing-tools-2026): AI image editing has matured beyond gimmicks. Here's what's actually useful in 2026: the tools, the techniques, and the workflows that integrate into real creative work.
- [Fine-Tuning vs. Prompting: Which Should You Use?](https://zro2.one/learn/llms-fine-tuning-vs-prompting): Before you invest in fine-tuning, make sure you actually need it. This guide breaks down when prompting is enough and when fine-tuning is the right call.
- [Few-Shot Prompting: Teaching Models with Examples](https://zro2.one/learn/prompting-few-shot-guide): Few-shot prompting is one of the most reliable techniques for getting consistent, high-quality LLM outputs. Here's how to use it effectively.
- [AI Video Understanding: Transcription, Summarization, and Analysis](https://zro2.one/learn/video-ai-understanding-and-analysis): AI can now extract meaningful information from video at scale. Here's what's practical in 2026: transcription pipelines, video summarization, content analysis, and the tools to build them.
- [Deep Learning Optimization in Practice — Getting Models to Train Faster and Better](https://zro2.one/learn/deep-learning-optimization-practical-guide): Practical techniques for stable deep learning training: optimizers, schedules, normalization, and debugging loss curves.
- [LLM Agents vs Chatbots — What Actually Changes in Product Design](https://zro2.one/learn/llms-agents-vs-chatbots-2026): A practical framework for deciding when a simple chatbot is enough and when you need an agentic architecture.
- [Data-Centric Machine Learning — A Playbook for Better Models Without Bigger Models](https://zro2.one/learn/machine-learning-data-centric-playbook-2026): How to improve ML performance by upgrading labels, coverage, and feedback loops before changing model architecture.
- [Video AI Workflows for Creators — Faster Production Without Losing Quality](https://zro2.one/learn/video-ai-workflows-for-creators-2026): A practical end-to-end video workflow using AI for ideation, editing, localization, and repurposing.
- [The Best AI Coding Assistants in 2026: A Practical Comparison](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-coding-assistants-2026): GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude, Gemini Code Assist — there are now dozens of AI coding assistants. Here's which ones are worth using and for what.
- [How to Build an AI Research Workflow: From Question to Answer, Faster](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-workflows-research-pipeline): A practical guide to using AI for research — from initial question through synthesis to reliable output. Real tools, real process, real pitfalls to avoid.
- [Voice Cloning in 2026: How It Works, What You Can Build, and What's Legal](https://zro2.one/learn/audio-ai-voice-cloning-2026): Voice cloning has gone from research demo to consumer product. Here's how it works, what you can legitimately build with it, and the legal and ethical lines you need to know.
- [Your First Week with AI: 7 Practical Exercises to Build Real Skills](https://zro2.one/learn/getting-started-first-week-exercises): The fastest way to get good at using AI isn't to read about it — it's to practice. Here are 7 exercises for your first week that build real, transferable skills.
- [AI Image Generation in 2026: Models, Tools, and What You Can Actually Build](https://zro2.one/learn/image-ai-generation-models-2026): From Midjourney to Flux to DALL-E 3 — the image generation landscape has changed dramatically. Here's where the models stand, what's actually good at what, and how to use them for real work.
- [MLLMs in Practice: What Vision-Language Models Can and Cannot Do in 2026](https://zro2.one/learn/mllms-in-practice-2026): Multimodal large language models can now see, hear, and read. Here's what they're actually good at in 2026, where they still fall short, and how to use them in real workflows.
- [System Prompts: The Hidden Instructions That Shape Every AI Response](https://zro2.one/learn/prompting-system-prompts-explained): Every AI assistant has a system prompt — hidden instructions that shape how it responds before you say a word. Here's what system prompts are, how they work, and how to write good ones.
- [AI Video Generation in 2026: What Sora, Runway, Kling, and Veo Can Do Now](https://zro2.one/learn/video-ai-generation-2026): AI video generation has moved from impressive demo to real production tool. Here's where Sora, Runway, Kling, and Google's Veo actually stand, what they're good for, and how to use them.
- [The Best AI Writing Tools in 2026: An Honest Guide](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-tools-for-writing-2026): Not all AI writing tools are created equal. Here's a no-hype breakdown of the best options in 2026 — what they're actually good at, where they fall short, and how to choose.
- [How to Build an AI Content Workflow (That Actually Saves Time)](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-workflows-content-team): A practical, step-by-step guide to building an AI-powered content workflow — from research through publishing. Real tools, real process, real time savings.
- [Audio AI in 2026: What It Can Do, What's Changed, and What to Use](https://zro2.one/learn/audio-ai-guide-2026): Audio AI has moved from novelty to essential tool. A comprehensive guide to what's possible in 2026: transcription, voice synthesis, music generation, and what to use for each.
- [30 Days to Actually Using AI: A Practical Learning Plan](https://zro2.one/learn/getting-started-ai-30-days): A structured 30-day plan for going from AI-curious to AI-capable. One small habit at a time. No tech background required.
- [Image AI in 2026: A Practical Guide for Creators and Builders](https://zro2.one/learn/image-ai-practical-guide): From zero to productive with image AI in 2026. What the tools can do, how to prompt effectively, which tool to use when, and what's still genuinely hard.
- [Context Windows Explained: Why LLMs Forget — And What to Do About It](https://zro2.one/learn/llms-context-windows-explained): Everything you send to an LLM fits inside a context window. Learn what that means, why it matters, and practical strategies for working within — and around — these limits.
- [Multimodal AI: What You Can Build When AI Sees, Hears, and Reads](https://zro2.one/learn/multimodal-ai-practical): AI that handles text, images, audio, and video simultaneously is changing what's buildable. A practical guide to multimodal AI use cases, tools, and workflows for 2026.
- [Advanced Prompting Techniques That Actually Work in 2026](https://zro2.one/learn/prompting-advanced-techniques): Move beyond basic prompting. A practical guide to chain-of-thought, few-shot learning, structured output, persona design, and meta-prompting — with real examples that produce measurably better results.
- [Video AI in 2026: What's Real, What's Useful, What's Coming](https://zro2.one/learn/video-ai-2026-guide): Video generation AI went from technically impressive to practically useful in 2025-2026. A grounded guide to what you can actually do, what the tools are, and where to start.
- [ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini (2026): Which One for What?](https://zro2.one/learn/chatgpt-vs-claude-vs-gemini-2026): A practical tool-selection guide: which model to use for writing, analysis, coding, and team workflows.
- [How to Build Your First AI Workflow in 60 Minutes](https://zro2.one/learn/how-to-build-ai-workflow-in-60-min): A step-by-step playbook to turn one repetitive task into a reliable AI-assisted workflow in one hour.
- [Machine Learning in the Real World — A Practical Playbook](https://zro2.one/learn/machine-learning-applied): How teams actually use ML in products: use cases, rollout strategy, metrics, and common failure modes.
- [Notion AI vs Coda AI vs Slite AI: Team Knowledge Tool Comparison](https://zro2.one/learn/notion-ai-vs-coda-ai-vs-slite-ai): Which AI-enabled knowledge tool is best for your team docs, collaboration style, and operating cadence.
- [Perplexity vs Google vs ChatGPT Search: Fast Guide](https://zro2.one/learn/perplexity-vs-google-vs-chatgpt-search): When to use each search mode for research, fact-checking, and decision-making without drowning in tabs.
- [Prompting That Actually Works (Without Overthinking It)](https://zro2.one/learn/prompting-that-actually-works): A practical prompting workflow you can use today for better answers, fewer retries, and less AI frustration.
- [How to Start Using AI Today — A Practical Guide](https://zro2.one/learn/start-using-ai-today): Ready to actually use AI? Here's exactly where to start, what to try first, and how to build AI into your daily life — starting today.

## Building with AI

- [AI Glossary: Builder's Edition](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-glossary-builders-edition): The terms you actually need when building AI-powered products: from inference basics to deployment patterns, defined plainly for people who ship things.
- [LLM Function Calling and Tool Use: A Developer's Guide](https://zro2.one/learn/llm-api-function-calling-guide): Function calling lets LLMs trigger real actions instead of just generating text. Here's how it works across major APIs, patterns that work, and pitfalls to avoid.
- [Multimodal Search: Finding Content Across Text, Images, and Audio](https://zro2.one/learn/multimodal-ai-search): Multimodal search lets you find images with text queries, match audio to descriptions, and bridge modalities. Here's how it works and how to build it.
- [Hybrid Search for RAG: Combining Dense and Sparse Retrieval](https://zro2.one/learn/rag-hybrid-search-guide): Pure semantic search often underperforms in production RAG systems. Hybrid search — combining dense embeddings with sparse retrieval — is the more reliable approach.
- [Designing Human-in-the-Loop AI Workflows That Scale](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-workflows-human-in-the-loop-design): Architecture patterns for AI workflows where humans review the right steps without becoming a bottleneck.
- [Audio AI Production Pipeline — From Raw Audio to Searchable Intelligence](https://zro2.one/learn/audio-ai-production-pipeline-guide): A practical architecture for speech transcription, speaker separation, summarization, and quality monitoring at scale.
- [Image AI Evaluation Guide — How Teams Measure Quality Beyond “Looks Good”](https://zro2.one/learn/image-ai-evaluation-guide-for-teams): A structured framework for evaluating image generation and vision systems with task-level metrics and review workflows.
- [LLM API Integration Reliability Checklist — 20 Controls Before Production](https://zro2.one/learn/llm-api-integration-reliability-checklist): A production checklist for LLM API integrations covering retries, guardrails, observability, and incident response.
- [MLLMs for Document Understanding — A Practical Playbook](https://zro2.one/learn/mllms-document-understanding-playbook): How to use multimodal LLMs for invoices, contracts, reports, and forms with accuracy and traceability.
- [Prompting as System Design — Patterns for Stable, High-Quality Outputs](https://zro2.one/learn/prompting-system-design-patterns): Prompt engineering patterns that treat prompts as maintainable system components rather than ad hoc text snippets.
- [RAG Evaluation and Guardrails — How to Keep Answers Useful and Grounded](https://zro2.one/learn/rag-evaluation-and-guardrails-guide): A practical guide to measuring RAG quality and implementing guardrails that reduce hallucinations in production.
- [Streaming LLM Responses: Why It Matters and How to Build It](https://zro2.one/learn/llm-api-streaming-responses): Streaming is how ChatGPT displays text as it's generated. Here's how it works under the hood, why it dramatically improves perceived performance, and how to implement it with the OpenAI and Anthropic APIs.
- [Evaluating RAG Systems: How to Know If Your Pipeline Is Actually Working](https://zro2.one/learn/rag-evaluation-guide): Building a RAG pipeline is straightforward. Knowing if it's actually working is hard. Here's a systematic approach to evaluating retrieval quality, generation quality, and end-to-end RAG performance.
- [LLM API Integration: A Complete Developer Guide](https://zro2.one/learn/llm-api-integration-guide): Everything you need to integrate LLM APIs into real applications: authentication, request patterns, streaming, error handling, cost management, and production best practices.
- [Feature Engineering: The Craft That Makes ML Models Actually Work](https://zro2.one/learn/machine-learning-feature-engineering): Better features beat better algorithms almost every time. A deep dive into feature engineering — the underrated craft at the heart of practical machine learning.
- [RAG in Production: Architecture Decisions That Actually Matter](https://zro2.one/learn/rag-production-architecture): Building a RAG system that works in production is harder than the demos suggest. A deep dive into the architecture decisions, failure modes, and engineering tradeoffs that determine whether your RAG actually works.
- [API Integration Patterns for LLM Features](https://zro2.one/learn/api-integration-patterns-for-llms): A technical guide to shipping LLM features safely: request shaping, guardrails, retries, and observability.
- [Machine Learning for Builders — Architecture, Trade-offs, and Deployment](https://zro2.one/learn/machine-learning-technical): A technical deep dive into the ML system lifecycle: data design, training, evaluation, serving, and reliability.
- [RAG for Builders: The Mental Model You Actually Need](https://zro2.one/learn/rag-for-builders-mental-model): A clear technical model for Retrieval-Augmented Generation: when to use it, where it fails, and what to measure.

## Futures & Research

- [Machine Learning Frontier — Open Problems That Actually Matter](https://zro2.one/learn/machine-learning-research): A research-level map of unresolved ML problems: generalization, robustness, data efficiency, causality, and alignment.
- [Multimodal AI — Frontier Research and Unresolved Problems](https://zro2.one/learn/multimodal-ai-research): The frontier of multimodal AI research: cross-modal alignment, grounding, emergent capabilities, compositional reasoning, evaluation methodology, and why integrating modalities is harder than it looks.

## Industry & Applications

- [AI Tools by Job Function — A Lean Stack That Actually Gets Used](https://zro2.one/learn/ai-tools-stack-by-job-function): A role-based method for selecting AI tools that people adopt, instead of collecting overlapping subscriptions.
- [Multimodal AI Product Patterns — Where It Creates Real User Value](https://zro2.one/learn/multimodal-ai-product-patterns-2026): Proven product patterns for combining text, image, audio, and video models in user-facing workflows.
